meta.name: Discipline & Rituals
meta.description: Daily startup, shutdown, focus rituals, reset routines, consistency loops, and anti-procrastination missions.

tasks.title: Morning Startup Ritual (5 min)
tasks.subject: Morning Startup
tasks.successCriteria: Top 3 missions selected, must-win chosen, first action defined
tasks.failureCondition: Starting the day reactively with no plan

steps.action: Open your mission board or task manager (30 sec)
Review calendar for today's commitments and deadlines (1 min)
Scan all active tasks and identify your top 3 priorities (1.5 min)
Select ONE must-win mission (the one that matters most) (30 sec)
Define the first micro-step for the must-win mission (30 sec)
Set a timer for your first focus block (30 sec)
Begin the first micro-step immediately (30 sec)

tasks.notes: This ritual prevents reactive days. Do it before checking email or social media. The first 5 minutes of your day determine the next 5 hours.

tasks.title: Evening Shutdown Ritual (10 min)
tasks.subject: Shutdown Routine
tasks.successCriteria: Day closed cleanly, tomorrow's first move prepared, workspace cleared
tasks.failureCondition: Ending the day in chaos with loose ends

steps.action: Review what you finished today (2 min)
Capture unfinished items and decide: do, delegate, defer, drop (3 min)
Pick tomorrow's first task (the one you'll do immediately) (1 min)
Save any open tabs, documents, or references for tomorrow (2 min)
Clear your physical workspace (1 min)
Close work applications and silence notifications (1 min)

tasks.notes: Shutdown rituals protect your mental health. Work is never truly 'done' - you just stop. This ritual helps you stop cleanly.

tasks.title: 5-Minute Anti-Procrastination Start
tasks.subject: Anti-Procrastination
tasks.successCriteria: 5 minutes of work completed on avoided task

steps.action: Identify the task you're avoiding (30 sec)
Set a timer for 5 minutes (15 sec)
Remove all distractions: phone face down, other tabs closed (30 sec)
Take one deep breath (15 sec)
Start the smallest possible first step (3 min)
When timer ends, decide: continue or stop? (30 sec)
Log what you accomplished (30 sec)

tasks.notes: The 5-minute rule is scientifically proven to overcome task paralysis. Momentum is the antidote to procrastination.

tasks.title: Weekly Reset Ritual (30 min)
tasks.subject: Weekly Reset
tasks.successCriteria: Week reviewed, wins identified, next week's top 3 missions set
tasks.recoveryMission: Pick 3 priorities for next week only

steps.action: Review daily logs from the past week (5 min)
List 5-7 wins and completed missions (5 min)
Identify 2-3 patterns or blockers that appeared multiple times (3 min)
Triage backlog: mark items as Keep, Drop, or Delay (5 min)
Archive or delete Drop items (2 min)
Set next week's top 3 missions (one per domain if applicable) (5 min)
Prepare Monday's must-win mission and first action (3 min)
Schedule next week's weekly reset (2 min)

tasks.notes: Weekly resets prevent drift. Do this even (especially) on weeks when you feel behind. Clarity is more valuable than productivity.

tasks.title: Energy Audit & Focus Check
tasks.subject: Energy Management
tasks.successCriteria: Energy level assessed, next break scheduled, focus adjusted
tasks.failureCondition: Pushing through low energy without adjustment

steps.action: Rate your energy level 1-10 (1 being exhausted, 10 being巅峰) (30 sec)
If energy < 5: take a 10-minute break (2 min to plan)
Drink a full glass of water (1 min)
Check posture: shoulders back, feet flat, screen at eye level (1 min)
Identify current focus level: deep, shallow, or scattered (30 sec)
Match next task to energy level (e.g., low energy = easy tasks) (30 sec)

tasks.notes: Working through low energy is not productive - it's burnout acceleration. Match task difficulty to energy level, not the other way around.
